root_text.sn47.40:1.1: “Satipaṭṭhānañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi satipaṭṭhānabhāvanañca satipaṭṭhānabhāvanāgāminiñca paṭipadaṁ.
root_text.sn47.40:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.40:1.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.40:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu samudayadhammānupassī kāyasmiṁ viharati, vayadhammānupassī kāyasmiṁ viharati, samudayavayadhammānupassī kāyasmiṁ viharati, ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.40:2.5: samudayadhammānupassī dhammesu viharati, vayadhammānupassī dhammesu viharati, samudayavayadhammānupassī dhammesu viharati, ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.40:3.3: sammādiṭṭhi, sammāsaṅkappo, sammāvācā, sammākammanto, sammāājīvo, sammāvāyāmo, sammāsati, sammāsamādhi.

translation_text.sn47.40:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you mindfulness meditation, the development of mindfulness meditation, and the practice that leads to the development of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.40:1.4: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.40:1.5: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.40:1.7: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.40:2.1: And what is the development of mindfulness meditation?
translation_text.sn47.40:2.2: It’s when a mendicant meditates observing the body as liable to originate, as liable to fall, and as liable to originate and vanish—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.40:2.5: principles as liable to originate, as liable to fall, and as liable to originate and fall—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.40:2.6: This is called the development of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.40:3.1: And what is the practice that leads to the development of mindfulness meditation?
translation_text.sn47.40:3.2: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn47.40:3.3: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn47.40:3.4: This is called the practice that leads to the development of mindfulness meditation.”
